title: actaea spicata subsp. rubra
intro: Actaea spicata subsp. rubra, also known as red baneberry, is a plant species belonging to the barberry family (Rosoideae) and is native to eastern North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Actaea spicata subsp. rubra include reducing fever and alleviating pain, as well as being used to treat various ailments such as rheumatism and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant typically include infusions of the roots and leaves, which can be used to make teas, tinctures, and salves.
